message:
fix(tests): replace exact f64 assert_eq with epsilon comparisons and clarify duration literal

Three floating-point test assertions in robot_types.rs used assert_eq! to
compare f64 values, which is fragile across platforms and optimization
levels due to IEEE 754 representation. Replaced with epsilon-based
comparisons using `(a - b).abs() < f64::EPSILON`:

- SearchData deserialization test: score field (1.5)
- EventsListData deserialization test: confidence field (0.95)
- AccountsListData deserialization test: percent_remaining field (85.5)

Also in storage_targets.rs, replaced `Duration::from_millis(5000)` with
the equivalent but clearer `Duration::from_secs(5)` in the indexing lag
red-threshold test — no behavioral change, just readability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
